evals.prompt: Analyze an escaped defect where a discount was not applied for a non-USD currency. Classify it and identify which test level should have caught it.
expected_patterns: Root cause category: Logic error OR Integration failure
Should-catch level: Unit OR Integration
Classification: [describes dimension 1, 2, or 3]
Prevention opportunity: Add test OR Improve existing test

evals.prompt: Run a 5 Whys analysis for a payment API that was called twice on form submit, causing double charging.
expected_patterns: 5 steps numbered 1-5
Why 1 OR Why 2 ... Why 5
reaches systemic cause OR process gap OR structural issue
stops at process/tool/structure, not 'developer forgot'
final action item: something the team can change
anti_patterns: stops at individual error level (Why 3 or earlier)
root cause is 'human error' without digging deeper

evals.prompt: Analyze a test suite where the escaped bug rate is increasing. Classify 10+ escaped bugs by root cause category, area, and should-catch level to identify patterns.
expected_patterns: Total count: [number]
By root cause: [categories with counts and %]
By area: [feature areas with counts]
By should-catch level: [unit/integration/e2e/monitoring]
Top action themes: [1-3 systemic fixes with bug counts they address]
anti_patterns: action items that are broad rather than scoped to high-impact themes

evals.prompt: Conduct a quarterly test suite health review. Assess flaky test trends, execution time, coverage gaps, and quarantine inventory.
expected_patterns: Flaky test rate (current % and trend over 3 months)
Top 5 flakiest tests with root cause
Execution time (current and 3-month trend)
Coverage gaps (overall % and critical paths)
Quarantine inventory (count, oldest age, resolution rate)
anti_patterns: no root cause explanation for flaky tests
no comparison to targets (<2% flaky, <14 days quarantine)

evals.prompt: Create a concrete action item from a postmortem with all required fields: deliverable, owner, deadline, and verification method.
expected_patterns: specific deliverable (e.g., 'add integration tests for payment API')
named owner (not 'team' or 'someone')
due date (specific sprint or date, not 'soon')
verification method: PR merged OR tests passing OR metric improved
anti_patterns: vague deliverable like 'improve testing' or 'be more careful'
scope too large for one sprint

evals.prompt: Facilitate a quality retro meeting for Sprint 14. Review previous action items, present metrics, identify needs, run root cause on top issue, and define new action items.
expected_patterns: Section: Previous Action Items (status: Done/In Progress/Dropped)
Section: Data Review (escaped bugs, flaky rate, CI pass rate, coverage)
Section: What Went Well
Section: What Needs Improvement (specific pain points with data)
Section: 5 Whys on top issue OR root cause discussion
Section: New Action Items (1-3 with owner/due date each)
anti_patterns: no review of previous items (breaks accountability loop)
action items too vague or too many (>3)
no owner or deadline on new items

evals.prompt: Write a quality incident postmortem for a P0 production bug where user data was corrupted. Include timeline, root cause, 5 Whys, test gaps, and immediate/short-term/long-term action items.
expected_patterns: Summary: one paragraph with what/who/how resolved
Severity and Impact section
Timeline table with UTC times
Root Cause section
5 Whys (5 steps to systemic cause)
What Tests Existed section
What Tests Were Missing section
Immediate/Short-term/Long-term actions (owner/due/status)
Lessons Learned
anti_patterns: 5 Whys stops early at individual action
no distinction between immediate and long-term actions

evals.prompt: Audit your postmortem action items and explain why they failed to close. Propose fixes for: no owner, no due date, scope too big, no review at next retro.
expected_patterns: Identifies: no owner OR no due date OR scope too big OR no review loop
For each reason, proposes specific fix
Fix for 'no owner': assign to named person with context
Fix for 'no due date': specific sprint or date
Fix for 'scope too big': breaks item into smaller PRs
Fix for 'no review': calendar slot for review at next retro
anti_patterns: doesn't connect fix to concrete mechanism (e.g., calendar reminder)

evals.prompt: Propose improvement cycle solutions for flaky tests with effort estimates (Small/Medium/Large). For each, discuss tradeoffs: manual adherence vs. automation, time to implement vs. long-term benefit.
expected_patterns: Solution A (Small — hours/1 day): quick fix with + and − tradeoffs
Solution B (Medium — 1 sprint): automation with tradeoffs
Solution C (Large — 2+ sprints): comprehensive with tradeoffs
+ indicates benefit (quick, low maintenance, automated, etc.)
− indicates cost or limitation
Recommendation: which to start with and sequencing

evals.prompt: Explain how to measure postmortem improvement using two metrics together. What happens if one is high and the other is low?
expected_patterns: Metric 1: defect escape rate (did similar bugs reappear?)
Metric 2: action-item-closure rate (% of items shipped on time)
Scenario: high closure rate + rising escape rate => wrong work
Scenario: low closure rate => postmortems are theater
Both metrics required, not one alone
mentions measurement after implementation (not just action)
anti_patterns: measures action item closure without escape rate context
treats closure rate as sufficient proof of success
